---
title: ""
page-layout: full
---

::: hero-wrapper
::: hero-text
# Connor Conkey-Morrison

::: hero-tags
[PhD Candidate]{.tag} [Provisional Psychologist]{.tag} [Sessional Academic]{.tag}
:::

::: hero-bio
I am a PhD candidate at RMIT University (School of Health and Biomedical Sciences). I also work as a provisional psychologist, and sessional academic at RMIT University and the Cairnmillar Institute.
:::

::: hero-links
[Research →](pages/research.qmd){.hero-btn .btn-primary} [Education & Experience →](pages/history.qmd){.hero-btn .btn-secondary} [Get in touch →](pages/contact.qmd){.hero-btn .btn-secondary}
:::
:::

::: hero-photo
![Connor Conkey-Morrison](images/crop_27A5334.jpg){.profile-img}
:::
:::

------------------------------------------------------------------------

::: about-section
## Current focus

I research school attendance problems and emotionally based school avoidance (EBSA) --- why children disengage from school, what interventions work, and how disengagement develops across childhood and adolescence. My work combines systematic review methods, longitudinal cohort modelling, and population-level education data.
:::

::: stats-strip
::: stat-item
**🔄**\
**6**\
Current papers in progress ️
:::

::: stat-item
**🔰**\
**4**\
peer-reviewed publications
:::

::: stat-item
**↗️**\
**2024--2026**\
active publication period
:::

::: stat-item
**✅**\
**PROSPERO**\
systematic review registered
:::
:::

::: card
### Research & Publications

Published work spanning social media, coping, and gaming. Works in progress: systematic review of EBSA interventions, longitudinal trajectory modelling with LSAC, and population-level attendance analysis.

[Read more →](pages/research.qmd){.card-link}

### Education & Experience

Sessional teaching at RMIT and Cairnmillar Institute. School counselling at Sacred Heart Primary School, Kew. Full academic, clinical, and professional history.

[Where have I been? →](pages/history.qmd){.card-link}

### Contact

Current appointments, academic profiles, and the best way to get in touch for research, teaching, or clinical inquiries.

[Get in touch →](pages/contact.qmd){.card-link}
:::
